content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Chain-of-Thought Prompting to Mitigate Social Biases in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) routinely generate outputs exhibiting social biases and stereotypes, especially in open-ended or under-specified prompts. Standard prompting strategies often fail to suppress these biases reliably, leading to unsafe or unfair generations.",
  "motivation": "Mitigating social bias in LLM outputs is critical for ensuring fairness, trust, and societal acceptance as these models are increasingly deployed in sensitive domains. Existing debiasing efforts mostly focus on post-processing or finetuning, but scalable, generalizable, and prompt-based mitigation is underexplored. Prompt engineering offers a lightweight, model-agnostic intervention point that can be rapidly deployed as models evolve.",
  "method": "We propose 'Counterfactual Chain-of-Thought Prompting' (CCoT): each user prompt is augmented with an explicit instruction to first generate a brief chain-of-thought reasoning about possible social biases or stereotypes relevant to the context, then to write a counterfactual scenario that inverts demographic or social attributes, and finally to produce the target output while considering both the original and counterfactual contexts. The prompt template ensures the LLM surfaces and actively counterbalances potential biases before output generation, leveraging its reasoning abilities and knowledge of diverse perspectives.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CCoT on standard stereotype and bias benchmarks (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs) and real-world prompt sets. Baselines will include standard prompting, instruction-tuning prompts, and self-reflection prompts. Metrics will include bias scores, toxicity/risk, and task accuracy to assess both bias reduction and output quality."
}
